content.title: 写 (xiě) — to write
content.body:
The verb **写** (xiě) means "to write."

**Pronunciation:** xiě (third tone, low dipping)

**Common usage:**
- 写字 (xiě zì) — to write characters
- 写汉字 (xiě Hànzì) — to write Chinese characters

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 写 + Object

Examples:
- 我写汉字。(Wǒ xiě Hànzì.) — I write Chinese charac

content.title: 做 (zuò) — to do, to make
content.body:
The verb **做** (zuò) means "to do" or "to make."

**Pronunciation:** zuò (fourth tone, sharp falling)

**Common usage:**
- 做饭 (zuò fàn) — to cook (make a meal)
- 做工作 (zuò gōngzuò) — to do work

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 做 + Object

Examples:
- 我做饭。(Wǒ zuò fàn.) — I cook.
- 他做工作。(Tā zuò g

content.title: 学习 (xué xí) — to study, to learn
content.body:
The verb **学习** (xué xí) means "to study" or "to learn."

**Pronunciation:** xué xí (second tone + second tone)

**Common usage:**
- 学习中文 (xué xí Zhōngwén) — to study Chinese
- 学习汉字 (xué xí Hànzì) — to study Chinese characters

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 学习 + Object

Examples:
- 我学习中文。(Wǒ

content.title: 工作 (gōng zuò) — to work
content.body:
The verb **工作** (gōng zuò) means "to work." It's a two-character compound that functions as a verb.

**Pronunciation:** gōng zuò (first tone + fourth tone)

**Usage:**
工作 can be both a verb (to work) and a noun (work/job).

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 工作

Examples:
- 我工作。(Wǒ gōng zuò.) — I

content.title: 开 (kāi) — to open, to drive
content.body:
The verb **开** (kāi) has two main meanings: "to open" and "to drive" (vehicles).

**Pronunciation:** kāi (first tone, high and level)

**Common usage:**
- 开门 (kāi mén) — to open a door
- 开车 (kāi chē) — to drive a car

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 开 + Object

Examples:
- 我开门。(Wǒ kāi mén.) —

content.title: Verb Choice in Communication
content.body:
Select the appropriate verb for different communicative situations.

**Context matters:**
Different situations call for different activity verbs. Choosing the right verb shows you understand both the meaning and appropriate usage.

**Examples:**
- At a restaurant: 吃饭, 喝水, 买 (but not 睡觉 or 工作)
- At a

content.title: Verb-Object Combinations
content.body:
Many Mandarin verbs naturally pair with specific objects to form common expressions. Learning these **verb-object collocations** helps you sound natural.

**Common fixed combinations:**
- 吃饭 (chī fàn) — eat rice/meal (not just "eat rice" but "eat a meal")
- 喝水 (hē shuǐ) — drink water
- 看书 (kàn shū)

content.title: 喝 (hē) — to drink
content.body:
The verb **喝** (hē) means "to drink." It pairs with liquids and beverages.

**Pronunciation:** hē (first tone, high and level)

**Common usage:**
- 喝水 (hē shuǐ) — to drink water
- 喝茶 (hē chá) — to drink tea
- 喝咖啡 (hē kāfēi) — to drink coffee

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 喝 + Object

Example

content.title: 说 (shuō) — to speak, to say
content.body:
The verb **说** (shuō) means "to speak" or "to say."

**Pronunciation:** shuō (fourth tone, falling)

**Common usage:**
- 说话 (shuō huà) — to speak / talk
- 说中文 (shuō Zhōngwén) — to speak Chinese
- 说英文 (shuō Yīngwén) — to speak English

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 说 + Object

Examples:
- 我说中

content.title: Daily Routine Description
content.body:
Use multiple activity verbs to describe a daily routine.

**Sample routine:**
早上我喝水。(Zǎoshang wǒ hē shuǐ.) — In the morning I drink water.
我吃饭。(Wǒ chī fàn.) — I eat.
我工作。(Wǒ gōng zuò.) — I work.
晚上我看书。(Wǎnshang wǒ kàn shū.) — In the evening I read books.
我睡觉。(Wǒ shuì jiào.) — I sleep.

Notice how ea

content.title: 买 (mǎi) — to buy
content.body:
The verb **买** (mǎi) means "to buy" or "to purchase."

**Pronunciation:** mǎi (third tone, low dipping)

**Common usage:**
- 买书 (mǎi shū) — to buy books
- 买水 (mǎi shuǐ) — to buy water
- 买东西 (mǎi dōngxi) — to buy things / shop

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 买 + Object

Examples:
- 我买书。(Wǒ mǎi

content.title: Activity Recognition in Context
content.body:
Apply your knowledge of activity verbs to understand descriptions.

**Scenario understanding:**
When you hear or read about someone's activities, you need to recognize which verbs describe which actions and understand the overall situation.

**Example passage:**
我是学生。早上我学习中文。我看书，写汉字，说中文。中午我吃饭，喝水。下午我

content.title: Distinguishing Similar Verbs
content.body:
Some HSK 1 verbs have overlapping meanings and require careful distinction.

**看 (kàn) vs. 读 (dú):**
- **看书** — to read silently, using your eyes
- **读书** — to read aloud, using your voice, or to study formally

Both can translate as "read," but 看 emphasizes visual reading, while 读 emphasizes vocali

content.title: Building Simple SVO Sentences
content.body:
Mandarin follows a **Subject-Verb-Object (SVO)** word order, similar to English.

**Basic pattern:**
Subject + Verb + Object

**Examples with different subjects:**
- 我看书。(Wǒ kàn shū.) — **I** read books.
- 你喝水。(Nǐ hē shuǐ.) — **You** drink water.
- 他吃饭。(Tā chī fàn.) — **He** eats.
- 她学习中文。(Tā xué xí

content.title: Two-Character Compound Verbs
content.body:
Some HSK 1 verbs are **two-character compounds** that function as single units.

**Two-character verb compounds:**
- **睡觉** (shuì jiào) — to sleep
- **工作** (gōng zuò) — to work
- **学习** (xué xí) — to study

**Structure notes:**
- These compounds must be kept together
- Unlike verb-object combination

content.title: 看 (kàn) — to watch, to read, to look at
content.body:
The verb **看** (kàn) is versatile, meaning "to watch," "to read" (silently), or "to look at."

**Pronunciation:** kàn (fourth tone, sharp falling)

**Common usage:**
- 看书 (kàn shū) — to read (a book)
- 看电视 (kàn diànshì) — to watch TV
- 看电影 (kàn diànyǐng) — to watch a movie

**Basic sentence pattern:

content.title: 吃 (chī) — to eat
content.body:
The verb **吃** (chī) means "to eat." It's one of the most essential daily activity verbs in Mandarin.

**Pronunciation:** chī (first tone, high and level)

**Common usage:**
- 吃饭 (chī fàn) — to eat (a meal) / eat rice
- 吃面 (chī miàn) — to eat noodles
- 吃鱼 (chī yú) — to eat fish

**Basic sentence pat

content.title: 听 (tīng) — to listen
content.body:
The verb **听** (tīng) means "to listen" or "to hear."

**Pronunciation:** tīng (first tone, high and level)

**Common usage:**
- 听音乐 (tīng yīnyuè) — to listen to music
- 听老师 (tīng lǎoshī) — to listen to the teacher

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 听 + Object

Examples:
- 我听音乐。(Wǒ tīng yīnyuè.)

content.title: 读 (dú) — to read aloud
content.body:
The verb **读** (dú) means "to read aloud" or "to study."

**Pronunciation:** dú (second tone, rising)

**Common usage:**
- 读书 (dú shū) — to study / read aloud
- 读汉字 (dú Hànzì) — to read Chinese characters aloud

**Distinction from 看:**
- 看书 — to read silently (using eyes)
- 读书 — to read aloud (using

content.title: Creating Action Sequences
content.body:
Combine multiple activity verbs to create logical action sequences.

**Natural sequences:**
Some activities naturally follow others:
- 买 → 吃 (buy → eat)
- 看书 → 写 (read → write, in a study context)
- 工作 → 睡觉 (work → sleep, end of day)

**Unnatural sequences:**
Some orders don't make logical sense:
-

content.title: 睡觉 (shuì jiào) — to sleep
content.body:
The verb phrase **睡觉** (shuì jiào) means "to sleep." Unlike most verbs, this is a two-character compound.

**Pronunciation:** shuì jiào (fourth tone + fourth tone)

**Usage:**
睡觉 is always used as a complete unit. Don't separate the characters.

**Basic sentence pattern:**
Subject + 睡觉

Examples:
-
